knockout_status.note: clinched/eliminated are EXACT and apply the OFFICIAL FIFA 2026 tiebreakers (see 'tiebreakers'): every remaining group result is enumerated (top 2 per group plus the 8 best of the 12 third-place teams reach the Round of 32), and teams level on points are separated HEAD-TO-HEAD FIRST, then by overall goal difference. A team is ELIMINATED only if it makes the Round of 32 in NO completion, CLINCHED only if in EVERY one -- ties are resolved so neither list can be wrong (where the only remaining decider would be card-conduct or FIFA ranking, which are not in the data, the call is left open, not guessed). Once the group stage is COMPLETE (every game finished and the Round of 32 bracket settled), the third-place finishers who missed the best-8 cut are also added to 'eliminated' -- the pre-whistle enumeration leaves them open because they could reach the Round of 32 in SOME completion, but once the standings actually settle they're out. Once the KNOCKOUTS begin, 'eliminated' also includes any team that LOST a knockout match before the semifinals (Round of 32 / Round of 16 / Quarterfinals) -- read straight from results (extra time / penalties included), never from the simulator. 'in_the_money' lists the teams that REACHED THE SEMIFINALS = the (up to 4) PAYING teams; a semifinal or final loser STAYS in_the_money (3rd, 4th and runner-up all cash) and is never called out. 'placement' fills in champion / runner_up / third / fourth as those games finish. on_the_brink lists still-alive teams whose chance of reaching the knockout bracket is under 20% under the selected forecast, most-endangered first. PRESENTATION (DLD style): a team is 'eliminated' ONLY if it is in the eliminated list; a team in on_the_brink is still ALIVE -- describe one whose chance rounds to ~0% as 'effectively out (~X% to advance), but not yet mathematically eliminated', never just 'eliminated'. SYMMETRICALLY, in the GROUP STAGE a team is 'IN / THROUGH to the Round of 32 / clinched' ONLY if it is in clinched; a still-contesting team that is NOT in that list -- even one whose reach odds the sim ROUNDS TO 100% -- has NOT clinched: describe it as 'effectively through (~X%), but not yet mathematically clinched -- it can still be caught', and say what would lock it (e.g. avoid losing its last group game); NEVER say a not-yet-clinched team 'is in' / 'is through' / 'has qualified', even at 100% -- 100% is the Monte-Carlo rounding up a 99.9%, not a settled berth. In the KNOCKOUTS, 'advanced' means a team has WON its CURRENT-round game and so is through to the NEXT round (the IN badge); mid-round it lists ONLY the sides already decided, NOT every still-alive team -- a team whose current-round game has not been played yet is NEITHER 'advanced' NOR 'eliminated': it is STILL ALIVE and contesting the current round (describe it as 'still alive in the [round], hasn't played its game yet', never 'through', never 'out'). Keep the settled call (clinched/eliminated/advanced/in_the_money, from the math) separate from the forecast chance.

rules: DLD is the DLD World Cup Contest. 26 people paid the $25 entry, so the prize pot is $650; 25 of them designated teams and 1 paid but made NO picks. So 625 points are spread across teams, but the pot is $650 -- the no-pick entry's $25 is in the pot yet, backing nothing, can win nothing, so by the orphan rule it flows to the actual winners (the active boards split the full $650). The 25 active entrants are the ones in the standings. In phase 1 they spread 20 points across teams; 5 more knockout points are added at phase 2 (June 28, 2026), for 25 points total each. Points are a PARI-MUTUEL stake, and ONLY THE FOUR SEMIFINALISTS pay -- reaching the semifinals is 'the paying round'. The pot is split by finish: the CHAMPION's backers split 50% of the $650, the RUNNER-UP's backers 30%, 3rd place 15%, 4th place 5%. Within each of those four teams, its share is divided among that team's backers in proportion to their points. ORPHAN RULE: the whole pot is always paid out -- if a paying place is taken by a team nobody backed, that place's share is redistributed across the places that ARE backed (so backing a semifinalist can pay more than its nominal slice); only if none of the four semifinalists were backed by anyone does that outcome pay no one. 'Chance to win' = probability of being DLD's single TOP earner (#1). 'Average payout' is expected dollars back (so profit = payout - $25). 'Cash' / 'break even' = winning back at least the $25 entry.
tournament_format: Tournament format (2026): 48 teams in 12 groups of 4. Each team plays 3 group-stage matches (round-robin within its group). ALL group matches are played -- none are skipped, even a 'dead rubber' where one team has already clinched advancing and the other is already eliminated -- and the result still matters, affecting final group position (1st vs 2nd changes the knockout path), goal difference, and the best-third-place rankings that decide the last knockout berths and the bracket seeding. The top 2 of each group PLUS the 8 best third-place teams -- 32 in all -- advance to the knockouts: Round of 32, then the Round of 16, then the Quarterfinals, then the Semifinals, and the Final (with a third-place match). Knockouts are single elimination. The Round of 32 bracket SLOTS are fixed in advance (each slot is 'winner of group X', 'runner-up of group Y', or 'a best third-place team'), so a specific matchup is known once both its slots are clinched. Groups finish on a ROLLING schedule, so SOME round-1 pairings -- those between confirmed finishers of groups that have already wrapped up -- can be known BEFORE the final group games end; but any matchup involving a best-third-place team is NOT set until ALL groups finish (the 8 best thirds, and which slot each fills, depend on every group's third-place record). So until the group stage fully completes the bracket is only PARTIALLY known. DLD's paying round is the semifinals: the four teams that reach the final four are the only ones that pay out.
tiebreakers: OFFICIAL FIFA 2026 GROUP-STAGE TIEBREAKERS. When two or more teams in a group finish LEVEL ON POINTS, their order is decided by, in sequence: (1) most points in the matches AMONG the tied teams (head-to-head); (2) goal difference in those head-to-head matches; (3) goals scored in those head-to-head matches; (4) goal difference in ALL group matches; (5) goals scored in all group matches; (6) team conduct / fair-play score (fewest disciplinary points from yellow/red cards); (7) FIFA world ranking. Criteria for head-to-head come BEFORE overall goal difference in this order. It means a team that beat, or out-scored head-to-head, the team it is level with finishes above it no matter the overall goal difference; if a subset is still level after the head-to-head criteria those criteria are re-applied among just that subset. The 8 BEST THIRD-PLACE teams (of the 12 groups) are ranked head-to-head-free (they are in different groups) by: (1) points, (2) goal difference, (3) goals scored, (4) team conduct score, (5) FIFA world ranking. NOTE: the data feed has results but not card counts or FIFA rankings, so clinched/eliminated are settled only where the listed criteria decide them; a tie that would reach conduct or FIFA ranking is left open, never guessed.
key_facts: FORMATTING: reply in plain conversational text only -- NO Markdown, NO asterisks or **bold**, NO bullet lists, NO headings, NO emoji. Just a sentence or two of normal prose. NAMING: always call this competition 'DLD' (its name) -- never 'the pool', 'the contest', or 'the prediction pool'. (The shared prize money can be called 'the pot'.) DATES: 'today' is the actual current date -- use it for 'today/tomorrow/this week'. 'as_of' is the date of the most recent FINISHED match (results are current through then) and is often a day or two BEFORE today; never call 'as_of' today, and don't assume the last result happened today. For specific games use recent_results / upcoming_fixtures with their own dates. Numbers come from a Monte-Carlo simulation of the rest of the tournament, conditioned on games already played, under the forecast named in 'forecast' (the same one the reader picked in the dashboard's selector). If asked which forecast these reflect, say so. They shift as results come in. For a head-to-head 'does X beat Y?' (X finishing with more money), read head_to_head['X > Y']. The key's direction IS the answer's direction: head_to_head['A > B'] is A's chance to out-earn B, and head_to_head['B > A'] is the reverse. Use the exact full names as written. The 'scenarios' give each entrant's chance to finish #1 in DLD assuming a pivotal team wins it all or misses the semis. 'title_odds_history' tracks each contender's win-the-cup % across checkpoints, so you can answer whether a team's odds moved and in which direction. 'recent_results' lists finished matches with scores (e.g. 'Mexico 1-1 South Korea') and the date. For 'who leads Group X / the current group order / who must still win', use 'group_standings'[Group X] -- the computed table, already sorted in OFFICIAL FIFA 2026 order: points, then head-to-head points among the tied teams, then head-to-head goal difference, then head-to-head goals scored, then overall goal difference, then goals scored; the top 2 advance. Never infer group order from raw scores. The full tiebreaker sequence is in 'tiebreakers'; cite it for 'what are the tiebreakers / how is a tie broken / what if two teams finish level?'. For 'who has clinched / who's through / who's IN the Round of 32 / who's eliminated / who's still alive', use 'knockout_status' -- its 'clinched' and 'eliminated' lists are EXACT (computed by enumerating every remaining group result), so state them as fact and do NOT re-derive them from the standings or from reach %s; 'advanced' = the dashboard 'IN' badge = in the GROUP STAGE the teams that CLINCHED the Round of 32, and in the KNOCKOUTS the teams that have WON their CURRENT-round game (through to the NEXT round) -- so mid-round 'advanced' is ONLY the sides already decided, NOT every still-alive team; 'in_the_money' = reached the semis, 'on_the_brink' lists still-alive teams in danger. A team not in any list is alive but not yet clinched/advanced or in danger. PRESENTATION (DLD style): a team is 'eliminated' ONLY if it is in 'eliminated' (describe a ~0% one as 'effectively out (~X% to advance), but not yet mathematically eliminated', never just 'eliminated'). SYMMETRICALLY, a group-stage team is 'IN / through / clinched / qualified for the Round of 32' ONLY if it is in 'clinched'. If it is NOT, it has NOT clinched -- even if its reach odds read 100% (the sim rounds a 99.9% up to 100%): say 'effectively through (~X%), but not yet mathematically clinched -- it can still be caught' and what would lock it. Leading its group in 'group_standings' is NOT the same as clinched. In the KNOCKOUTS a team is 'through to the next round' ONLY if it is in 'advanced' (it has already won its current-round game); a still-alive team whose current-round game has not been played yet is NEITHER through NOR out -- it is 'still alive in the [round], hasn't played yet'. Keep the settled call (from the math) separate from the forecast chance. 'upcoming_fixtures' is the ENTIRE remaining schedule (every not-yet-played match with a set date, group games through the final; knockout slots may read 'TBD vs TBD' until the bracket fills). For 'when does TEAM play next?', read next_match_by_team[TEAM] (date, time, opponent 'vs'). Each team row has p_round32_pct (chance to get out of the group into the knockout bracket), p_semis_pct (reach the final four / paying round), and p_win_cup_pct. Each entrant has payout_if_team_reaches_semis = the expected dollars they'd win if a given backed team reaches the semis. Each entrant also has best_case_payout = the most they could still win if their still-alive teams take the four paying spots -- bracket-feasible (a reachable semifinal foursome), using their current share of each team; use it for 'what's my best case / the most I could possibly win' and give the exact dollar figure rather than re-deriving it. For 'where did people put their extra/incremental/final/knockout 5 points', 'who added phase-2 points to TEAM', or 'what did NAME bet in the knockout round', use the 'phase2' block: phase2.by_player[NAME] is that entrant's 5 knockout points as {team: points}, and phase2.by_team ranks teams by phase-2 points received. board_points (per entrant) and pool_stake (per team) are the 20+5 TOTALS, so isolate the extra 5 via phase2, NOT board_points. If 'phase2' is absent, the knockout points are not public yet -- do not reveal or guess them. Otherwise only answer questions about DLD and this tournament from the data provided; politely decline unrelated topics, and do not invent causes or advice the data does not support.

phase2.note: The 5 knockout-stage points each of the 25 entrants added at phase 2 (locked June 28, 2026), spread across the 32 round-1 knockout qualifiers. These are INCREMENTAL -- on top of each player's 20 group points. board_points (per entrant) and pool_stake (per team) elsewhere are the 25-pt TOTALS, so to answer 'where did the EXTRA/incremental/final/knockout 5 points go' use THIS block, not board_points. by_player[name] = that entrant's 5-point knockout allocation {team: points}; by_team ranks teams by total phase-2 points received.

contest.name: DLD
contest.forward_note: the remaining rounds will decide the payouts
